[2018-05-11 22:21] dertgor (Barley): Hey guys just found out about prismata! Seems like my type of game. Im a bit confused on how to get it. What's the deal with these keys?
[2018-05-11 22:22] liadahlia (Lia): go to https://whosgamingnow.net/giveaway/freeprismata
[2018-05-11 22:22] liadahlia (Lia): they should have some free keys left
[2018-05-11 22:24] dertgor (Barley): Thx bro. One question -- why does the game cost money on steam if people can get it for free?
[2018-05-11 22:24] jamesa7171: the $25 price tag includes lots of cosmetic stuff and bonus single-player content
[2018-05-11 22:24] dertgor (Barley): Ah makes more sense now
[2018-05-11 22:25] dertgor (Barley): I was reading in #ask_a_dev that people got scammed on whosgamingnow
[2018-05-11 22:25] dertgor (Barley): Is that a valid fear?
[2018-05-11 22:26] jamesa7171: there was some confusion yesterday because a bunch of people came in here saying they got invalid keys from that site, and we've seen some shady stuff going on with prismata keys on some sites so the conclusion for most people was that it was some kind of scam
[2018-05-11 22:27] jamesa7171: it turns out they got the keys from elyot and that it was legitimate, just a bug which caused duplicate keys to be sent out
[2018-05-11 22:27] jamesa7171: any key you get from that site should be legitimate (unless they ran out again?) but if you have problems getting a key there pm me and i can give you one
[2018-05-11 22:32] dertgor (Barley): Ok thx! Appreciate it!
[2018-05-11 22:33] dertgor (Barley): Also sry for spamming this chan with questions about keys. I know it can get quite annoying 😅
[2018-05-11 22:34] jamesa7171: it's all good, don't worry about it 👍
[2018-05-11 22:37] awaclus: >Thx bro. One question -- why does the game cost money on steam if people can get it for free?
also it's only in early access/beta testing for now, and the devs want to avoid leaving bad first impressions on the people who are not very dedicated, so this is their way of getting the more dedicated players to play for now, or something like that

[2018-05-13 01:09] oceanbrz: Brand new player here, just got a key yesterday after listening to Neon’s interview with Elyot . Want to ask a question here after playing through the first campaign and combat training:
Is the ladder/casual fairly matched? I am still learning the game and a little bit worried about getting trounced in my first few games against humans... where should I start and how I can build confidence about my play?
[2018-05-13 01:11] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): casual is definitely fine
[2018-05-13 01:12] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): they insert bots in the casual queue that matches your level (and you prolly couldnt tell bots and human players apart as of now)
[2018-05-13 01:13] amalloy: i mean, you will get trounced in your first few games against humans, even if you build your confidence first
[2018-05-13 01:13] amalloy: but you can get plenty of practice against bots, either in quick play or by turning up the casual match settings to prefer bots
[2018-05-13 01:14] totally_not_fbi (Totally Not FBI): yeah, practising against bots will certainly help
[2018-05-13 01:16] ilyak (Ilya K): yeah the learning curve is more like a learning cliff
[2018-05-13 01:17] ilyak (Ilya K): feels like it's going to be a while before someone can play the "real game", even if they have experience playing all three of Starcraft, Chess, and the grindiest midrange matchups ever

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: when you start a ticket, for every game you win, you get a certain amount of ticks

messages.content: once you have enough ticks (aka complete the ticket) you get rewards based on what ticket it was
